hypothesis: Incorporating uncertainty quantification into the LLM's tool-use loop—by having the simulator return confidence intervals derived from propagated uncertainty in the activation energy parameter Ea rather than single-point phase fraction predictions—will significantly improve LLM synthesis planning success rates on multi-phase target challenges (analogous to Challenges 2 and 3) by enabling the agent to distinguish thermodynamically robust waypoints from sensitive ones and preferentially route synthesis trajectories through low-uncertainty regions of the phase diagram.
